LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DAVID STEWART'S MOTION TO VACATE AND SET ASIDE RENEWAL OF JUDGMENT, RECALL AND QUASH WRIT OF EXECUTION AND RETURN OF PROPERTY LEVIED UPON. A R G U E D. THE COURT ADOPTS THE TENTATIVE RULING. DEFENDANT DAVID STEWART'S MOTION TO VACATE THE RENEWED JUDGMENT ENTERED AGAINST HIM ON AUGUST 2, 2016 IS GRANTED. DEFENDANT HAS MET HIS BURDEN TO SHOW THAT HE IS ENTITLED TO RELIEF UNDER CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 683.170 ON THE GROUND THAT HE WAS NEVER PROPERLY SERVED WITH THE SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT IN THE UNDERLYING ACTION. FIDELITY CREDITOR SERVICE, INC. V. BROWNE (2001) 89 CAL.APP.4TH 195, 200-203. PLAINTIFF SIMMONS FIRST NATIONAL BANK HAS NOT SHOWN THAT IT COULD NOT WITH REASONABLE DILIGENCE HAVE PERSONALLY DELIVERED A COPY OF THE SUMMONS AND COMPLAINT TO DEFENDANT SUCH THAT SUBSTITUTE SERVICE ON DEFENDANT AT A PRIVATE OR COMMERCIAL POST OFFICE BOX WOULD HAVE BEEN PROPER PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE SECTION 415.20(B). THE PROCESS SERVER'S DECLARATION OF DILIGENCE REFLECTS THAT HE ATTEMPTED TO SERVE DEFENDANT ONLY AT THE LOCATION OF THE PRIVATE MAILBOX FACILITY. CONTRARY TO PLAINTIFF'S ARGUMENT, WHETHER THE DEFENDANT HAD A MERITORIOUS DEFENSE TO THE UNDERLYING ACTION IS NOT A RELEVANT CONSIDERATION. FIDELITY CREDITOR SERVICE, INC., 89 CAL.APP.4TH AT 204-206. TO THE EXTENT THAT DEFENDANT'S MOTION SEEKS TO RECALL AND QUASH UNSPECIFIED WRITS OF EXECUTION OR ABSTRACTS OF JUDGMENT AND TO ORDER THE RETURN OF ANY PROPERTY LEVIED PURSUANT TO THE RENEWED JUDGMENT, IT IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. COUNSEL FOR DEFENDANT TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED ORDER THAT IS VERBATIM WITH THE COURT'S RULING IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM

calendar.matters:
MOTION FOR SET ASIDE/VACATE JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jan-22-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Gary Bemis, Esq. 951-588-2080 for Plaintiff through Court Call.
Christopher Peters, Esq. 888-922-2867 of the Fullman Firm for Defendant

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court shares the basis for the tentative ruling. Plaintiff argues proof of service, no other address. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT DAVID STEWART's MOTION TO VACATE AND SET ASIDE RENEWAL OF JUDGMENT, RECALL AND QUASH WRIT OF EXECUTION AND RETURN OF PROPERTY LEVIED UPON.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling. Defendant David Stewart's motion to vacate the renewed judgment entered against him on August 2, 2016 is granted. Defendant has met his burden to show that he is entitled to relief under Code of Civil Procedure section 683.170 on the ground that he was never properly served with the summons and complaint in the underlying action. Fidelity Creditor Service, Inc. v. Browne (2001) 89 Cal.App.4th 195, 200-203. Plaintiff Simmons First National Bank has not shown that it could not with reasonable diligence have personally delivered a copy of the summons and complaint to defendant such that substitute service on defendant at a private or commercial post office box would have been proper pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure section 415.20(b). The process server's declaration of diligence reflects that he attempted to serve defendant only at the location of the private mailbox facility. Contrary to plaintiff's argument, whether the defendant had a meritorious defense to the underlying action is not a relevant consideration. Fidelity Creditor Service, Inc., 89 Cal.App.4th at 204-206. To the extent that defendant's motion seeks to recall and quash unspecified writs of execution or abstracts of judgment and to order the return of any property levied pursuant to the renewed judgment, it is denied without prejudice.

Counsel for Defendant to submit a proposed order that is verbatim with the Court's ruling in compliance with CRC 3.1312.(as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
